page: Microsoft Azure DC32eds_v5 is a dedicated virtual machine instance belonging to the DCEDV5 family. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The configuration features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type includes 957 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile is designed to balance memory capacity with local storage performance, offering a high-density resource allocation for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution.

The DC32eds_v5 is engineered for memory-optimized workloads, database deployments, and enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated physical resource allocation. Its qualitative cost efficiency is defined by its dedicated CPU allocation and integrated local storage, which reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32eds_v5 is a dedicated virtual machine instance from the DCEDV5 server family, utilizing an Intel x86_64 processor architecture and the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance includes 957 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it does not feature any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for memory-optimized and database workloads, the DC32eds_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency through its dedicated CPU allocation and substantial local storage capacity, which minimizes the reliance on external storage for high-throughput operations. It is suitable for hosting relational databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive processing tasks.
